healthy.title: Healthy Plant
healthy.description: Your plant looks healthy and is growing well.
treatment: Continue your current care routine as the plant is healthy.
Water only when the top 1 to 2 inches of soil feel dry to avoid overwatering.
Clean leaves gently with a damp cloth to remove dust and improve photosynthesis.
Inspect the plant weekly for early signs of pests or disease.

leafspot.title: Leaf Spot Disease
leafspot.description: Dark or brown spots appearing on leaves, often caused by bacteria or fungi.
treatment: Carefully remove and dispose of all infected leaves using clean scissors to stop the spread.
Avoid watering from above; always water directly at the soil level to keep leaves dry.
Apply a mild fungicide or neem oil spray every 7 to 10 days until symptoms reduce.
Ensure good airflow around the plant by spacing it properly or moving it to a ventilated area.
Disinfect gardening tools after use to prevent spreading the infection to other plants.

blight.title: Blight Disease
blight.description: Rapid browning and death of plant tissues, often spreading quickly.
treatment: Immediately prune and remove all affected parts of the plant to prevent rapid spread.
Apply a strong fungicide suitable for blight diseases, following instructions carefully.
Reduce watering frequency and ensure the soil is well-draining to avoid excess moisture.
Move the plant to a location with better airflow and moderate sunlight.
Avoid touching healthy plants after handling infected ones without washing hands or tools.

powderymildew.title: Powdery Mildew
powderymildew.description: White powdery coating on leaves caused by fungal infection.
treatment: Gently wipe off the white powder from leaves using a soft cloth or tissue.
Spray neem oil or a baking soda solution (1 tsp baking soda + 1 liter water) every 5 to 7 days.
Place the plant in a brighter area with indirect sunlight to slow fungal growth.
Increase air circulation by keeping space between plants or using a fan indoors.
Remove heavily infected leaves if the infection is widespread.

rust.title: Rust Disease
rust.description: Orange or yellow powdery spots on leaves caused by fungal infection.
treatment: Remove and discard infected leaves showing orange or yellow powdery spots.
Avoid misting or wetting the leaves, as moisture promotes fungal growth.
Apply a fungicide or neem oil spray every 7 days until the condition improves.
Keep the plant in a well-ventilated area with good airflow.
Regularly check nearby plants and isolate the infected plant to prevent spreading.

unknown.title: Condition Not Detected
unknown.description: System is not able to detect condition for now. Follow normal plant care routine.
treatment: Water the plant only when the top soil feels dry.
Provide adequate sunlight based on plant type.
Ensure proper air circulation around the plant.
Remove dry or damaged leaves regularly.
Monitor plant condition for a few days.
